macro.title: Rocket Mortgage, Rocket Pro adopt VantageScore 4.0
macro.what: Rocket began using VantageScore 4.0 with Classic FICO, as FHFA pilots the model and lenders delivered $10 million in loans to Freddie Mac.
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Pending Home Sales Slip for First Time Since Early April as Mortgage Rates Climb
macro.what: Mortgage rates jumped to a 10-month high, spooking would-be homebuyers and sellers after a few weeks of solid demand
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Iran Tensions, Not Economic Data, Are Driving Mortgage Rates This Week
macro.what: This Week In A Nutshell: Rates are likely to move more on headlines out of Iran than on economic data
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: The MRED Zillow legal fight just hit agents and sellers directly
housing.what: MRED cut Zillow and Trulia listing feeds amid an antitrust suit, pulling 40,000 listings after 9 were banned under Zillow display standards.
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

housing.title: Single-family housing starts fell in April, but multifamily gained momentum
housing.what: April housing starts rose 4.6% year over year while single-family starts fell 2.4%, and new home inventory declined 4.6% from last year.
housing.why: Supply pipeline sets future inventory and construction activity.
housing.market: Stronger pipeline aids suppliers; oversupply later can weigh on pricing.

housing.title: AI adoption in mortgage servicing is accelerating, BlackWolf says
housing.what: BlackWolf says AI adoption rose from 15% in 2023 to 38% in 2025, cutting mortgage servicing costs by 30% to 50% amid more oversight.
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Why housing construction can’t grow at current demand levels
housing.what: April housing starts ran at 1.465 million SAAR and single-family fell to 930,000 as completed new homes for sale held at 121,000.
housing.why: Supply pipeline sets future inventory and construction activity.
housing.market: Stronger pipeline aids suppliers; oversupply later can weigh on pricing.

housing.title: Smaller mortgage vendors face squeeze from lenders, regulators
housing.what: Mortgage vendors are entering a new phase of consolidation as they respond to rising regulatory and cybersecurity pressures, according to a white paper released this week by invest
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Home Purchase Cancellations Are No Longer on the Rise As Demand Ticks Up
housing.what: Contract cancellations declined slightly in April as homebuyers and sellers gained a clearer sense of the housing market after years of volatility, and as demand picked up
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

housing.title: Coming Soon in Chicago: A $2M Move-In Ready Duplex Just Steps From Lake Michigan
housing.what: In this edition of Coming Soon–a series from Redfin News that explores new listings that house hunters can find early only on Redfin.com and Compass.com–we’re exploring a duplex unit in Chicago’s Lincoln Park
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

housing.title: Prices Drops Are Becoming Slightly Less Common As Housing Market Stabilizes
housing.what: The share of home sellers cutting their asking prices has fallen from its peak as buyers lose a bit of negotiating power, but price drops are still much more common than in years past
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: U.S. Home Prices Rose 0.2% in April
housing.what: Improving homebuyer demand could fuel further price gains in the coming months
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
housing.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.

notables.title: As retirement costs surge, more homeowners turn to their equity
notables.what: 65-and-older households now spend about $122,000 annually, compared with roughly $60,000 in 2000, according to survey data.
notables.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
notables.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

notables.title: New brokerage Showings bets on buyer leads over listing commissions
notables.what: Showings said its aims to modernize the selling experience through a fee-free structure "centered on savings, efficiency and buyer activity."
notables.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
notables.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

notables.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.

notables.title: Consolidation isn’t the crisis real estate brokers think it is — but it is a wake-up call
notables.what: Brokerage mergers can increase ad and recruiting spend, pressuring agents who rely on company leads and brand recognition.
notables.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
notables.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.
